description_long: This is a robust, single-bladed axe featuring a broad, angular metal blade with a slightly beveled edge and a pronounced poll. The blade is attached to a thick, cylindrical handle that appears to be made of wood, reinforced at both ends with dark metallic bands. The handle is slightly curved, providing ergonomic grip and control, and the top is capped with a pointed metal spike, adding a secondary striking or piercing function. The axe head is finished in a dark, glossy metal, showing some wear and subtle texturing, while the handle has a matte, natural wood appearance. The overall design suggests durability and utility, suitable for heavy-duty tasks in urban environments such as demolition or emergency rescue. No visible branding or decorative engravings are present, emphasizing a utilitarian aesthetic.
description: A heavy-duty, single-bladed axe with a dark, glossy metal head and a reinforced, slightly curved wooden handle capped with metal bands and a pointed spike.
description_view_0: Front view shows the pointed metal spike at the top and the cylindrical handle with metal bands, with the blade edge barely visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the broad, angular blade and the ergonomic curve of the handle, as well as the reinforced metal bands.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the poll of the axe head and the full length of the handle, with the spike and metal bands clearly visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view reveals the blade's thickness, the handle's curvature, and the robust attachment of the head to the handle.
attribute_handle_type: Slightly curved cylindrical wooden handle, reinforced with dark metal bands at both ends, ergonomic grip, round cross-section.
attribute_blade_design: Single broad, angular blade with a beveled edge and pronounced poll, dark glossy finish, secondary pointed spike on top.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged and banded mounting with visible metal reinforcement at the head and base.
